questions.prompt: <span aria-label="Students design an experiment to determine the unknown dielectric constant kappa of a plastic material. A capacitor is created using two square aluminum plates of side length s equals 30 centimeters that are separated by a distance d equals 1.0 millimeter . This capacitor is placed in a circuit with an ideal 6.0-volt battery, a resistor of resistance R equals 500 ohms , voltmeter V, and an open switch S, as shown above. A 1.0 millimeter thick piece of plastic is inserted between the aluminum plates. The distance x that the plastic is inserted between the plates can be varied, and the voltmeter is used to measure the potential difference V sub C across the capacitor. The switch is closed, and readings from the voltmeter are recorded as a function of time t. The data are plotted to create the graph shown below. The figure presents the graph of a curve in a plane with the origin labeled O. The horizontal axis is labeled t. Just to the right of the origin is a tick mark labeled t sub one half. The vertical axis is labeled V sub C, in volts, and the numbers 3 and 6 are indicated. The curve begins at the origin and moves steadily upward and to the right at first. It passes through the point with coordinates t sub one half, comma 3, and continues upward with a decreasing rate until it starts to level off as it approaches V sub C equals 6. The time t sub one half shown above is the time for the capacitor to charge to half the potential difference of the battery. open parenaclose paren The potential difference across the capacitor as a function of time is modeled by the equation V s ub C equals V sub max, end subscript, times, open parenthesis, 1 minus e raised to the negative t over R C, power, close parenthesis , where V sub max equals 6 volts . Derive an expression for the capacitance C of the capacitor. Express your answer in terms of t sub one half , R, and physical constants, as appropriate. Unauthorized copying or reuse of any part of this page is illegal. -6- The data for x and t sub one half are recorded for several trials and the value of C for each trial is calculated. The results are shown in the chart below. x, in meters 0.050 0.10 0.15 0.20 0.25 t s u b o n e half, in microseconds 0.44 0.63 0.75 0.88 1.10 C, in nanofarads 1.27 1.82 2.16 2.54 3.17 open parenbclose paren Plot the experimental value of the capacitance C as a function of the distance x on the graph below. Clearly scale and label all axes, including units if appropriate. Draw a straight line that best represents the data. The figure presents a 25 by 20 grid. The gridlines are dashed lines. There are bolder dashed lines drawn every 5 grid units in the horizontal and vertical directions. open parencclose paren The capacitor in the lab can be treated as two capacitors in parallel, one with the dielectric and one with air between the plates. Show that the capacitance can be expressed as C equals, the fraction epsilon sub 0, end subscript, times S, and denominator d, end fraction, times, open parenthesis, s plus, x times open parenthesis, kappa minus 1, close parenthesis, close parenthesis. . Question 1 continues on the next page. Unauthorized copying or reuse of any part of this page is illegal. -7- open parendclose paren Using the graph from part open parenbclose paren, calculate the value of the dielectric constant kappa . open pareneclose paren The students now want to verify the value for the permittivity constant, epsilon sub 0 . Using the graph from part open parenbclose paren, calculate an experimental value for epsilon sub 0 . open parenfclose paren Assume the value found in part open pareneclose paren is higher than the accepted value for the permittivity constant. State one possible physical reason for this error and explain how it could have caused this error.
